teaching_arc.entry_point: Welcome back, Topic 16 hunted critical points where f′=0. Today we prove such points (or parallel secant tangents) are guaranteed when hypotheses hold.
teaching_arc.progression: Road-trip intuition → hypothesis checklist (Rolle vs MVT) → formal theorems → worked c-values → corner trap → pause practice → applications → visual labs → recap teasing Topic 18.
teaching_arc.destination: Students verify hypotheses, find MVT values of c, and explain geometry (horizontal vs parallel tangent).

topic_goal: Teach Rolle's Theorem and the MVT with theorem statements, worked c-problems, geometry via secant_tangent and flex_plot, and hypothesis traps.
topic_takeaways: State Rolle's and MVT hypotheses and conclusions
Verify hypotheses before applying either theorem
Find c guaranteed by MVT on concrete intervals
Use Rolle/MVT in short proofs (roots, constant functions)
Interpret horizontal vs parallel tangent geometry
style_notes: Welcome back, do not re-introduce Arian from scratch.
Callback Topic 16 critical points and f′(c)=0; tease Topic 18 derivative tests on summary.
theorem_box on s03 Rolle and s06 MVT, read hypotheses slowly.
misconception_compare s02 Rolle vs MVT; s08 corner breaks differentiability.
math_solution_steps on s04, s07, s10, s11, s12, cue step_1..step_n in order.
secant_tangent s06, s09, s15: narrate a then h as timeline moves; call slope readout.
flex_plot: unique plot per slide, probe and guides at c.
visual_lab s14/s15: YouTube demo one tab ~30s; site gets all three.
pause_and_reveal on s09 (real answer c=1) and s16 exit check.
proof_sketch optional on s05 Rolle, s13 MVT→constant.
transition_style: Metaphor → formal theorem → worked example → student pause → application → lab → recap.

slide_ref.source_title: Rolle's Theorem and the Mean Value Theorem

slides.narration_seed: Welcome back to Arian University. Topic 16 found where f prime is zero; today we prove those horizontal tangents must exist when Rolle's hypotheses hold, and generalize to the Mean Value Theorem.
Five objectives: state both theorems, verify hypotheses, find c, short proofs, and read the geometry.
slides.transition_to_next: Road-trip metaphor on equal endpoint heights.

slides.narration_seed: Same start and end elevation on a smooth road, somewhere you drive level. flex_plot x squared minus four on minus two to two.
misconception_compare: Rolle needs equal endpoints; MVT does not. Both need differentiability on the open interval, corners break the theorems.
theorem_box: continuous, differentiable, f(a)=f(b) implies f prime of c equals zero. Graph parabola with horizontal guide at c=2.
math_solution_steps: verify polynomial, f(1)=f(3)=0, solve 2c-4=0, c=2 in (1,3). step_1 through step_5.
Optional proof_sketch: EVT gives extrema; equal endpoints force interior extremum; Fermat gives f prime zero.
theorem_box MVT. secant_tangent on x squared: vary a and h until secant slope matches tangent, parallel lines at c≈1.
f(x)=x cubed minus 2x on [-1,2]. Average slope 1. 3c squared minus 2 equals 1 gives c=1; discard endpoint c=-1.
|x-1| continuous on [0,2] but corner at 1, MVT fails. Average slope 0 but derivative never 0.
Pause: f(x)=x squared on [0,2]. Find c. pause_and_reveal answer c=1. secant_tangent full interval.
Reveal: average slope 2, f prime 2x, c=1. math_solution_steps step_1..step_4.
Constant f: average slope 0, f prime zero everywhere, any c works. Non-unique c is allowed.
IVT gives a root; Rolle between two roots would force f prime zero, contradiction since f prime always positive.
Optional: f prime zero everywhere, MVT between any a,b gives f(b)=f(a), so constant.

slide_ref.source_title: Lab: Rolle's Theorem on a Parabola

slides.narration_seed: visual_lab three intervals on x squared minus 4x plus 3. Demo tab B. Horizontal tangent at x=2 when endpoints match.

slide_ref.source_title: Lab: MVT, Match Secant and Tangent Slopes

slides.narration_seed: visual_lab secant_tangent: presets a=0,h=2 then narrow to a=1,h small. Slopes match at c≈1.
Recap Rolle vs MVT. Check differentiability. pause_and_reveal exit. Next: Topic 18 derivative tests.
slides.transition_to_next: Topic 18 First and Second Derivative Tests.
